---
title: "Prediction space"
description: "Join prediction draws to covariates with prediction_draws()"
status: stable
---

`prediction_draws()` joins posterior-predictive draws (e.g. `mu`) to a covariate grid, producing one tidy row per `chain × draw × observation`. Parameters are deliberately **excluded** — prediction space stays separate from parameter space, so coefficients never get duplicated across observations.

With `newdata=None` it reads covariates straight from a constant-data group in the `DataTree`; pass a `DataFrame` to use a custom grid. It fails loudly if that group is missing. The result is an eager `DataFrame`, so call `.filter()` to subset before plotting.

## Setup

A hierarchical regression with 4 groups of 20 observations. The `DataTree` carries a `posterior` group, a `predictions` group (`mu`), and a `constant_data` group with the covariates `x`, `group`, and the observed `y`.

```{python}
#| code-fold: true
import numpy as np
import polars as pl
import arviz as az
import tidydraws as td
from xarray import DataArray
from lets_plot import (
    LetsPlot,
    aes,
    as_discrete,
    geom_line,
    geom_point,
    geom_ribbon,
    ggplot,
    labs,
    theme_minimal,
)
LetsPlot.setup_html()

np.random.seed(42)
chains, draws, n_groups = 4, 500, 4
n_per, total = 20, 80
group = np.repeat(np.arange(n_groups), n_per)
x = np.random.normal(10, 5, total)

true_int = np.random.normal(0, 0.5, n_groups)
true_slope = np.random.normal(1.0, 0.2, n_groups)
y = np.array([true_int[g] + true_slope[g] * xi + np.random.normal(0, 0.8)
              for g, xi in zip(group, x)])

intercept = np.random.normal(0, 0.5, (chains, draws, n_groups))
beta = np.random.normal(1.0, 0.2, (chains, draws, n_groups))
mu = np.zeros((chains, draws, total))
for i in range(total):
    g = group[i]
    mu[:, :, i] = intercept[:, :, g] + beta[:, :, g] * x[i]

dt = az.from_dict(
    {
        "posterior": {
            "intercept": DataArray(intercept, dims=["chain", "draw", "groups"]),
            "beta": DataArray(beta, dims=["chain", "draw", "groups"]),
        },
        "predictions": {"mu": DataArray(mu, dims=["chain", "draw", "obs_ind"])},
        "constant_data": {
            "x": DataArray(x, dims=["obs_ind"]),
            "group": DataArray(group, dims=["obs_ind"]),
            "y": DataArray(y, dims=["obs_ind"]),
        },
    },
    coords={
        "groups": [f"g{i}" for i in range(n_groups)],
        "obs_ind": list(range(total)),
    },
    dims={"intercept": ["groups"], "beta": ["groups"],
          "x": ["obs_ind"], "group": ["obs_ind"], "y": ["obs_ind"]},
    pred_dims={"mu": ["obs_ind"]},
)
```

One call joins `mu` to `x`, `group`, and `y`:

```{python}
pred = td.prediction_draws(
    dt, newdata=None, var_name="mu", constant_data_group="constant_data"
)
pred.head()
```

Note the columns: `chain`, `draw`, `obs_ind`, `x`, `group`, `y`, `mu`. No `beta`, no `intercept` — parameters stay in their own frame.

## Gallery

### Posterior predictive fit by group

Summarise `mu` per observation with quantiles, then a ribbon + median line.

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Posterior predictive fit by group (89% credible ribbon)."
summary = (
    pred.group_by("obs_ind", "x", "group", "y")
    .agg(
        pl.col("mu").quantile(0.055).alias("lower"),
        pl.col("mu").median().alias("median"),
        pl.col("mu").quantile(0.945).alias("upper"),
    )
    .sort("x")
)

(
    ggplot(summary.to_pandas(), aes("x"))
    + geom_ribbon(aes(ymin="lower", ymax="upper", fill=as_discrete("group")), alpha=0.25)
    + geom_line(aes(y="median", color=as_discrete("group")), size=0.8)
    + labs(x="x", y="mu", color="group", fill="group",
           title="Posterior predictive fit by group")
    + theme_minimal()
)
```

### Fit with observed data overlaid

The observed `y` lives in the same tidy frame — overlay it as points.

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Posterior predictive fit with 89% credible ribbon and observed data."
observed = summary.select("x", "y", "group").unique()

(
    ggplot(summary.to_pandas(), aes("x"))
    + geom_ribbon(aes(ymin="lower", ymax="upper", fill=as_discrete("group")), alpha=0.2)
    + geom_line(aes(y="median", color=as_discrete("group")), size=0.8)
    + geom_point(
        data=observed.to_pandas(),
        mapping=aes("y", color=as_discrete("group")),
        size=1.2, alpha=0.6,
    )
    + labs(x="x", y="mu / y", color="group", fill="group",
           title="Posterior predictive fit with observed data")
    + theme_minimal()
)
```

### Filter before plotting

Because the result is an eager `DataFrame`, subset with `.filter()` before summarising — useful when the full prediction space is large.

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Posterior predictive fit for group 0, with observed data."
g0 = td.prediction_draws(
    dt, newdata=None, var_name="mu", constant_data_group="constant_data"
).filter(pl.col("group") == 0)

s0 = (
    g0.group_by("obs_ind", "x", "y")
    .agg(
        pl.col("mu").quantile(0.055).alias("lower"),
        pl.col("mu").median().alias("median"),
        pl.col("mu").quantile(0.945).alias("upper"),
    )
    .sort("x")
)

(
    ggplot(s0.to_pandas(), aes("x"))
    + geom_ribbon(aes(ymin="lower", ymax="upper"), fill="steelblue", alpha=0.3)
    + geom_line(aes(y="median"), color="navy", size=0.9)
    + geom_point(aes("y"), color="black", size=1.0, alpha=0.5)
    + labs(x="x", y="mu / y", title="Group 0 fit with observed data")
    + theme_minimal()
)
```

See [`parameter_draws()`](parameter_draws.qmd) for parameter space, or
[`compare_draws()`](compare_draws.qmd) for prior vs posterior.
